snp4.14:1.1: “I ask the Kinsman of the Sun, the Great Seer,
snp4.14:1.2: about seclusion & the state of peace.
snp4.14:1.3: Seeing in what way is a monk unbound,
snp4.14:1.4: clinging to nothing in the world?”
snp4.14:2.1: “He should put an entire stop to the root of objectification-classifications:
snp4.14:2.5: to subdue any craving inside him.
snp4.14:3.3: he shouldn’t, because of it, make himself hardened,
snp4.14:3.4: for that isn’t called unbinding by the good.
snp4.14:4.1: He shouldn’t, because of it, think himself better,
snp4.14:4.3: Touched by contact in various ways,
snp4.14:4.4: he shouldn’t keep theorizing about self.
snp4.14:5.2: a monk shouldn’t seek peace from another, from anything else.
snp4.14:5.4: there’s nothing embraced, so how rejected?
snp4.14:6.1: As in the middle of the sea
snp4.14:6.2: it is still, with no waves upwelling,
snp4.14:7.1: “He whose eyes are open has described
snp4.14:7.2: the Dhamma he’s witnessed, subduing danger.
snp4.14:7.3: Now tell us, sir, the practice:
snp4.14:8.1: “One shouldn’t be careless with his eyes,
snp4.14:8.2: should close his ears to village-talk,
snp4.14:8.4: or view anything in the world as mine.
snp4.14:9.3: shouldn’t covet anywhere any states of becoming,
snp4.14:10.3: he should not make a hoard.
snp4.14:10.4: Nor should he be upset when receiving no gains.
snp4.14:11.2: he should refrain from restlessness, shouldn’t be heedless,
snp4.14:11.4: should live in a noise-less abode.
snp4.14:12.3: he should abandon weariness, deception, laughter, sports,
snp4.14:12.4: sexual intercourse, & all that goes with it;
snp4.14:13.1: should not practice casting spells, interpret dreams, physical marks,
snp4.14:14.1: A monk shouldn’t tremble at blame
snp4.14:15.4: or flatter people in hope of gains.
snp4.14:17.3: shouldn’t despise others for their life, discernment,
snp4.14:18.4: for those who retaliate aren’t calm.
snp4.14:19.2: a monk inquiring should always train in it mindfully.
snp4.14:19.4: he shouldn’t be heedless of Gotama’s message—
snp4.14:20.2: witnessed the Dhamma, not by hearsay, but directly, himself.
snp4.14:20.3: So, heedful, you should always do homage & train in line with that Blessed One’s message,”
